name: python3-roman-numerals
title: python3-roman-numerals
summary: Python library for manipulating well-formed Roman numerals
description:
Python library for manipulating well-formed Roman numerals
Integers between 1 and 3,999 (inclusive) are supported. Numbers beyond this
range will return an OutOfRangeError.
.
The classical system of roman numerals requires that the same character may
not appear more than thrice consecutively, meaning that 'MMMCMXCIX' (3,999)
is the largest well-formed Roman numeral. The smallest is 'I' (1), as there
is no symbol for zero in Roman numerals.
.
Both upper- and lower-case formatting of roman numerals are supported, and
likewise for parsing strings, although the entire string must be of the same
case. Numerals that do not adhere to the classical form are rejected with an
InvalidRomanNumeralError.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-roman-numerals because Python library for manipulating well-formed Roman numerals.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-roman-numerals as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-roman-numerals with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-roman-numerals --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
